description: Ceri Smith is Director General, Office for Investment at the Department for Business and Trade (DBT). From April 2021, he was Director General, Strategy and Investment at the Department for International Trade ( DIT ). He joined DIT after 8 years at UK Government Investments where, among other things, he set up the British Business Bank, and led on the government’s shareholder interests in Channel 4, the Land Registry, Homes England and the Ordnance Survey. He was a Non-Executive Director at the British Business Bank and a Non-Executive member of the Homes England Board. Ceri previously spent almost 20 years across a range of economic policy roles in HM Treasury and the Business Department, in Brussels with the Foreign Office and with the DTI’s Small Business Service. Ceri is a former Trustee of the MS Society and spent 15 years as a governor of Camden School for Girls & Acland Burghley School. He is currently a governor of the Motability Foundation.

details.body:
Ceri Smith is Director General, Office for Investment at the Department for Business and Trade (DBT). From April 2021, he was Director General, Strategy and Investment at the Department for International Trade (DIT).

He joined DIT after 8 years at UK Government Investments where, among other things, he set up the British Business Bank, and led on the government’s shareholder interests in Channel 4, the Land Registry, Homes England and the Ordnance Survey. He was a Non-Executive Director at the British Business Bank and a Non-Executive member of the Homes England Board.

Ceri previously spent almost 20 years across a range of economic policy roles in HM Treasury and the Business Department, in Brussels with the Foreign Office and with the DTI’s Small Business Service.

Ceri is a former Trustee of the MS Society and spent 15 years as a governor of Camden School for Girls & Acland Burghley School. He is currently a governor of the Motability Foundation.

Ceri Smith is Director General, Office for Investment at the Department for Business and Trade (DBT). From April 2021, he was Director General, Strategy and Investment at the Department for International Trade (DIT).

He joined DIT after 8 years at UK Government Investments where, among other things, he set up the British Business Bank, and led on the government’s shareholder interests in Channel 4, the Land Registry, Homes England and the Ordnance Survey. He was a Non-Executive Director at the British Business Bank and a Non-Executive member of the Homes England Board.

Ceri previously spent almost 20 years across a range of economic policy roles in HM Treasury and the Business Department, in Brussels with the Foreign Office and with the DTI’s Small Business Service.

Ceri is a former Trustee of the MS Society and spent 15 years as a governor of Camden School for Girls & Acland Burghley School. He is currently a governor of the Motability Foundation.

The Office for Investment is a joint unit reporting to the Department for Business and Trade, HM Treasury and Number 10 Downing Street. It works across government to land top-tier investment into the UK.

role_appointments.title: Ceri Smith - Director General, Strategy and Investment

details.body:
Ceri Smith is Director General, Office for Investment at the Department for Business and Trade (DBT). From April 2021, he was Director General, Strategy and Investment at the Department for International Trade (DIT).

He joined DIT after 8 years at UK Government Investments where, among other things, he set up the British Business Bank, and led on the government’s shareholder interests in Channel 4, the Land Registry, Homes England and the Ordnance Survey. He was a Non-Executive Director at the British Business Bank and a Non-Executive member of the Homes England Board.

Ceri previously spent almost 20 years across a range of economic policy roles in HM Treasury and the Business Department, in Brussels with the Foreign Office and with the DTI’s Small Business Service.

Ceri is a former Trustee of the MS Society and spent 15 years as a governor of Camden School for Girls & Acland Burghley School. He is currently a governor of the Motability Foundation.

The Director General, Strategy and Investment is responsible for:

  • delivering a new investment strategy for DIT
  • providing oversight of the investment services function, including the Office for Investment, to ensure that the UK attracts the most strategic and impactful investments
  • leading our strategy teams
  • developing Department-wide positions on a range of policy issues as part of our new operating model

role.title: Director General, Strategy and Investment, DIT

role_appointments.title: Ceri Smith - Director General, Strategy and Investment

He joined DIT after 8 years at UK Government Investments where, among other things, he set up the British Business Bank, and led on the government’s shareholder interests in Channel 4, the Land Registry, Homes England and the Ordnance Survey. Ceri is a former Trustee of the MS Society and spent 15 years as a governor of Camden School for Girls & Acland Burghley School. He is currently a governor of the Motability Foundation.

Ceri Smith is Director General, Office for Investment at the Department for Business and Trade (DBT). From April 2021, he was Director General, Strategy and Investment at the Department for International Trade (DIT).

He joined DIT after 8 years at UK Government Investments where, among other things, he set up the British Business Bank, and led on the government’s shareholder interests in Channel 4, the Land Registry, Homes England and the Ordnance Survey. He was a Non-Executive Director at the British Business Bank and a Non-Executive member of the Homes England Board.

Ceri previously spent almost 20 years across a range of economic policy roles in HM Treasury and the Business Department, in Brussels with the Foreign Office and with the DTI’s Small Business Service.

Ceri is a former Trustee of the MS Society and spent 15 years as a governor of Camden School for Girls & Acland Burghley School. He is currently a governor of the Motability Foundation.

The Director of the Business Bank and Public Data Group (PDG) Unit is responsible for:

  • the successful creation of the British Business Bank
  • oversight of the Public Data Group
  • the government’s shareholder interest in the Ordinance Survey, HM Land Registry, Companies House and the Met Office

role.title: Director, Business Bank and Public Data Group (PDG) Unit, Shareholder Executive

title: Ceri Smith
